_note: Course manifest for 'History of Astrology' (pages/course.html + pages/course-viewer.html). Pattern mirrored from recursive-tarot/course/*, adapted: instead of hand-authored MDX chapters, this course reads its chapters LIVE from grammars/historiographies-of-astrology/grammar.json — the same grammar the Library already serves. Adding a chapter is a data edit in that grammar (add a new item[] whose id starts with chapterIdPrefix); this manifest only carries the course-level config + optional per-chapter companion links. Keep this in sync if the source grammar's item ids change.
title: History of Astrology
kicker: The Recursive Astrology · a dynamic course, read from the library itself
description: Five thousand years of astrological practice, era by era — Babylonian omen-craft to the contemporary resurgence — read directly from the Historiographies of Astrology grammar. Each chapter is one view honestly held in its own terms; where this repo has a matching primary-source grammar (Ptolemy, Alan Leo, Proctor’s critique, the flagship interpretation set), the chapter links onward to it.
appendixIntro: Three cross-cutting patterns the chapters above share, drawn from the same grammar — not another era, but a thread running through several of them.

companions._note: chapter item id -> array of grammar slugs (from ids.json) offered as 'Read the primary source' links. Chapters not listed here simply show no companion — an honest gap (no primary-source grammar yet in this repo), not a hidden fallback.
